note: Provenance Kit scan failed; treat as unverified.

disposition.rationale: BF16 fingerprinting workload (~60 GB) exceeds available unified memory on macbook-pro (32 GB) and mac-mini (16 GB). HF ID was already operator-confirmed pre-attempt; Cisco kit scan would have promoted operator_confirmed to verified-*, but operator-accepted SCAN_OOM is the doctrine-acknowledged next-best class when scan is hardware-blocked. Operational use of the MLX-3bit serving derivative is unaffected (12.5 GiB on disk, RSS 1.7 GB serving on macbook-pro 32 GB).
disposition.supplements_wrapper_record: These fields supplement the wrapper-written body. A future rescan (e.g. on Mac Studio) overwrites the wrapper-managed top-level fields; canonical disposition record lives in docs/_provenance/backfill-2026-05-10.md.

attempts.exit_mode: SIGKILL/OOM (process killed by OS during fingerprint computation)

upgrade_path.trigger: Mac Studio (96 GB unified memory) reachable post-Headscale-resolution
upgrade_path.expected_verdict: verified-specific OR verified-base-family (qwen--qwen family is in deep-signals catalog)
upgrade_path.promotion_doctrine: operator_confirmed → verified-{specific,base-family}; MLX-3bit derivative chain promotes from quantization-of-operator-confirmed-base → quantization-of-verified-base per D-17-92
